programme.title: Hoarders: Ingrid
programme.desc: Home shopping junky, Ingrid, has let her hoarding take over her home. Unopened boxes fill rooms to the point that it is impossible to use almost the entire house.

programme.title: Hoarders: Linda and Kerry
programme.desc: Linda hoarded out a 180-acre historic farm in preparation for the end of times and is facing the potential loss of her property to city authorities if she can't clean up. Kerry started hoarding plants after he lost his job as a military medic.

programme.title: Hoarders: Lonnie and Linda
programme.desc: Lonnie is a famed neurosurgeon and former Lt. Governor of the State of Nevada. He is also a notorious hoarder, spending an estimated $10 million on his collections. Linda's loved ones are on the verge of having authorities remove her from her home.

programme.title: Hoarders: Stanks and Anne
programme.desc: The Stanks have hoarded themselves out of their home and are facing the loss of their property if they cannot get it up to code. Anne's husband will leave her for his own physical safety if she doesn't clean up her Christmas hoard.

programme.title: Hoarders: Becky
programme.desc: Becky has hoarded her home and motel property with furniture, clothes and appliances. Now, she's hoarded herself out of the lives of her children, grandchildren, and her husband.

programme.title: Movie: Marauders
programme.desc: When a bank is hit by a brutal heist, three FBI agents are assigned to find the robbers. All of the evidence seems to point to the owner of the bank, until more banks are hit. The agents start to see a deeper purpose behind the bank robbers' actions.

programme.title: Movie: Run All Night
programme.desc: An aging hit man finds his loyalties tested when he's forced to kill the son of his longtime best friend. Now his own estranged son's life is in danger and he must set out on the run.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Out of Left Field
programme.desc: Find out whose familiar face showed up in the waiting room, how many dogs Sal had to babysit, and which Joker came up with the punishment that left Joe feeling numb.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Bogey Man
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes and discover who considered taking a quick thumbs down. Plus, find out what type of vacation Murr prefers, and how many attempts it took to film the punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Drawing a Blank
programme.desc: Find out what classic bit began in this early episode, which Jokers used to live together, and why filming in Times Square became a bad idea.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Out of TP
programme.desc: Find out why so many early-season challenges happened in a mall, how the team hid the cameras, and what Sal said that became an instant classic. Plus, what the crew considers to be one of the first iconic moments of the show.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Supercuts
programme.desc: Find out which entrepreneur sparked a Jokers fashion debate, when Joe first starting 'nosing' people and which challenge was originally filmed for the pilot episode.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Birds and the Bees
programme.desc: Learn what has become a signature part of Q's wardrobe and who among the Jokers is considered the best dancer. Plus, find out which character made his first appearance on an early episode of the show.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 8
programme.desc: Four friends compete to embarrass each other in the ultimate hidden camera showdown.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 3
programme.desc: Join the guys as they do what they do best: talk with their mouths full. The boys will surely be eating a well-balanced meal and dishing out what they've been up to at home.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 4
programme.desc: You're cordially invited to a one-of-a-kind dinner party at Joe, Q, Murr, and Sal's homes from the comfort of your own.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 7
programme.desc: Four friends compete to embarrass each other in the ultimate hidden camera showdown.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Like a Boss
programme.desc: Why the slow-moving line at The Fast and the Furious ride made things harder for the guys, who officiated Joe's wedding, and why last-minute changes were needed for Murr's Tumblr punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Truth Hurts
programme.desc: Find out which challenge became an instant classic, what act of kindness blew the crew away and what word the Jokers are responsible for in Urban Dictionary.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Dumbbell
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes on some of your favourite episodes.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: A Christmas Story
programme.desc: Lucy, Andy, Ike and Granny try to get Eddie into the Christmas spirit. Chief has a special gift that may just do the trick.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Lost Boy
programme.desc: Find out which joker once worked as a fish monger, who lied on his resume to snag a TV internship and what chain restaurant the guys used during Sal's 'shush!' punishment at the mall.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Chick Magnet
programme.desc: Find out which joker owned a bar in Staten Island, what had the crew crying laughing and whose dad grew up on a chicken farm.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Dumbbell
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes on some of your favourite episodes.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: A Christmas Story
programme.desc: Lucy, Andy, Ike and Granny try to get Eddie into the Christmas spirit. Chief has a special gift that may just do the trick.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Crickets
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Q and Joe head to Joey Fatone's hot dog joint to dish up weenies, and tonight's big loser performs a stand-up comedy routine but barely gets out alive.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Speech Impediment
programme.desc: Joe, Q, Sal and Murr crack each other up from behind glass, then test out awkward proposals. Plus, the big loser gets tongue-tied while speaking to government officials, and later, Joey Fatone goes behind the scenes on after party.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Pantsing With the Stars
programme.desc: The guys pair up to share educational ideas with perplexed parents, then teach Krav Maga to martial arts newbies. When one joker refuses, he becomes the big loser, and faces an opponent that scares the pants off him.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Silence of the Lame
programme.desc: It's all play and no work when the guys square off in a toy-pitching competition. And tonight's big loser has to quiet down patrons in a food court.

programme.title: The Misery Index: They Didn't Blur the Dog!
programme.desc: Find out why animals should never drive, and which NSYNC star makes a surprise appearance.

programme.title: The Misery Index: Send in the Clowns
programme.desc: See a moped crash that'd make Wylie Coyote proud, a big family surprise for one Tenderloin, and Murr makes a very bad bet.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Tooth and Consequences
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q spread the love as attendants in a bridal dress shop much to the dismay of the brides-to-be, and break a sacred guy-code in the process. Plus, they attempt to sway frightened focus groups with fragrance campaigns that stink.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Mime and Punishment
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q put the nasty in namaste when they lead guided meditations. Tonight's big loser gets boxed in with a 24-hour punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Find out why the food court was picked for the 'stalk and text' challenge, what classic comedy inspired 'over the shoulder,' and who was lucky enough to test out the flying contraption for Joe the Genie.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Flatfoot the Pirate
programme.desc: Who made the call to give Murr a face full of slushie at the mall? How did the Jokers keep their props secret from each other before Laugh Man Standing? And whose breast milk did Pirate Sal chug?

programme.title: Movie: Wild Wild West
programme.desc: Two government agents are brought together by the President to track down an evil genius.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Hitting the Wrong Note
programme.desc: The guys dress to the nines, but act like zeroes while trying to get sympathy, and tonight's big loser faces the music in a tone-deaf punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Ruffled Feathers
programme.desc: The guys conduct focus groups to sell their illogical inventions created by the other guys, then head to a shoe store where they get off on the wrong foot. Plus, tonight's big loser discovers he’s more flight than fight at the zoo.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Centaur of Attention
programme.desc: The guys go head-to-head in a focus group, and then spread some confusion at a bagel store before heading to the park where they hit all the wrong notes. Finally, tonight's biggest loser gets hunted down during a truly humiliating walk of shame.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Virtual Insanity
programme.desc: The guys bark up the wrong tree at a pet store, and tonight’s big loser virtually loses his mind after entering a world he’s never visited before.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr cruise the food court sending texts to strangers, then throw caution, and groceries, to the wind. And tonight's loser is cast as a genie in a play but wishes he could be anywhere else.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Remember the Pact
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q beg shoppers to hold on to a weird item, then teach tech to seniors. Tonight's loser reveals his embarrassing digital life to his dad.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: The C-Team
programme.desc: Station 24 is forced to work with the buff and handsome C-Team and their new transfer Delilah. Terry buys a gas-guzzling truck and Eddie takes him for a ride.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Fire Choir

programme.desc: Eddie heads a peer counseling session that leads the crew to learn more about themselves.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Ike and Mike
programme.desc: Ike's twin brother pays a visit to ask a favor. Meanwhile, the crew takes DNA tests and get some shocking results.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Firefighter's Ball (Part One)
programme.desc: Terry learns some new dance moves to impress Vicky at the fireman's ball, while Lucy discovers that her date is more like his mother than she realized. Plus, Eddie finds out he is up for a big promotion.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Firefighter's Ball (Part Two)
programme.desc: Things go off the rails for the crew as the Ball rages on. Teddy and Eddie find themselves stuck in an elevator, Ike deals with a drunk Andy, while Lucy struggles to find alone time with her date.

programme.desc: Author and Faith and Freedom Coalition founder Ralph Reed talks about the influence of Christians in American politics and discusses why they should support the re-election of President Trump. His books include "Politically Incorrect," and "Active Faith."

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Leave No One Behind
programme.desc: Julie becomes jealous as Tami Taylor's relationship continues to blossom with Tyra. Matt finds himself in a rut when nothing really seems to matter to him anymore.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: May the Best Man Win
programme.desc: Brian Williams receives what may be the opportunity of his life. When Tami Taylor runs into her ex-boyfriend from high school, she decides to stay in Dillon for a few days. Tim Riggins continues his pursuit of Lyla by getting his own radio show.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: I Knew You When
programme.desc: Brian Williams has graduated and the start of the new school year has Coach Taylor uncertain about the strength of his team. Matt's position as QB1 is in danger when a new freshman star player arrives on the scene.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Tami Knows Best
programme.desc: Smash struggles with his confidence on the field as Coach Taylor preps him for college tryouts. Meanwhile, Tami makes changes to the school budget and finds that the community cares more about a Jumbotron than education.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: The pressure builds for Coach Taylor to make some changes to his team for an upcoming game. Saracen also feels the heat as the fans lose faith in him and turn to the new quarterback. Meanwhile, Tim gets dragged into a risky business scheme.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Hello Goodbye
programme.desc: Tyra gets wooed by Cash, the new bad boy rodeo star in town and says goodbye to her relationship with Landry. After years of being away, Saracen's mother Shelby tries to re-enter his life. Smash leaves Dillon for the first time in his life.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Every Rose Has Its Thorn
programme.desc: Tyra's new love, Cash, seems to be the man of her dreams but that quickly changes when his dark side comes to light. Jason thinks he has a solution to bring him and Erin back together, but she has a very different plan in mind.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: It Ain't Easy Being J.D. McCoy
programme.desc: New quarterback J.D. McCoy becomes a victim of freshman hazing but begins to win the respect of the town and his team after he wins his first game. Meanwhile, Julie and Matt get closer after a night at the lake as Tyra and Cash drift farther apart.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: J.D. has gained popularity as the new star quarterback but his skills are put to the test at Friday night's big rival game. Meanwhile, Landry thinks he is finally over Tyra when he meets Devin, who seems like the perfect girl.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: New York, New York
programme.desc: Jason Street follows a job opportunity to New York in an effort to get his family back and brings Riggins along for the ride. As Cash gets ready to leave Dillon for his rodeo tour, Tyra is torn between following him or her college dream.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Game of the Week
programme.desc: All eyes are on Dillon as the Panthers' playoff game is chosen as the nationally televised 'high school game of the week.' Meanwhile, Riggins debates whether or not to meet with a college coach.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: The Giving Tree
programme.desc: Buddy Garrity has a run-in with the law and is forced to come clean about his financial troubles to Lyla. Landry agrees to tutor Tyra for the SATs, but he quickly reaches his breaking point.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: A Hard Rain's Gonna Fall
programme.desc: Saracen continues to struggle caring for his grandmother as her mental health rapidly deteriorates. Lyla is still reeling after discovering her father gambled away her college fund when she learns she has been accepted to the school of her dreams.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Underdogs
programme.desc: The friendship between the Taylors and McCoys is on thin ice after the scuffle between J.D. and Joe. Buddy tries to repair his relationship with Lyla while Landry and Tyra try to rekindle their romance one more time.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Tomorrow Blues
programme.desc: Five months after the state championship, everyone is getting ready from some big changes. Tim has second thoughts about college; Matt prepares to go to college and leave his grandmother and Julie behind; Landry and Tyra face an uncertain future.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: East of Dillon
programme.desc: Coach Taylor wonders how low his fortunes can sink as he tries to pull the East Dillon Lions together and Tami must navigate the new politics at West Dillon.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: After the Fall
programme.desc: A controversy at West Dillon puts star player Luke Cafferty on the Lion's roaster. Tami takes a stand which proves to be unpopular and Matt and Tim find an unexpected new living arrangement.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: The Skin of a Lion
programme.desc: Coach betrays Tami's trust when the Lions need new uniforms and tension between Vince and Luke threaten the team's chance on the field.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: A Sort of Homecoming
programme.desc: Homecoming at the newly reopened school prompts Coach Taylor to investigate East Dillon's history. Meanwhile, Tim and Matt bond during a spur of the moment hunting trip.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: The Son
programme.desc: Vince feels conflicted by the adulation he receives for being a star football player and for the criminal activity he engages in to support his family. Matt is having trouble dealing with his feelings about his father and Becky and Tom grow closer.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Stay
programme.desc: Facing the Lions' toughest opponent yet, Coach searches for a strategy to shut down its fabled juggernaut offence. Meanwhile, Julie and Matt attend a concert in Austin while Tami remain unaware of their actions.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: In the Bag
programme.desc: Coach thinks that Vince has brought a gun to school and Tami unwinds at a party with the West Dillon faculty.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: Toilet Bowl
programme.desc: The Lions play the worst team in the league, leading people to dub the mismatch 'The Toilet Bowl.' Meanwhile, Tami and Julie go on a college tour in Boston.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: The Lights in Carroll Park
programme.desc: Coach Taylor and Buddy crusade to make East Dillon park safer for the players. Tami is feeling distanced from her distracted husband.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights: I Can't
programme.desc: Coach Taylor is urged by Big Mary to find a new way to lead his team. When Becky goes to Tim for help, Tami ends up in an uncomfortable position. Vince is forced to deal with a medical emergency involving his mother.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Cart to Table
programme.desc: The chefs must use mislabelled items from the clearance carts to make comfort food. Next the chefs carry as many items as they can to make breakfast for dinner.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Global Grub
programme.desc: Guy is on the search for some awesome foreign flavours.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Multicultural Cooking
programme.desc: Guy digs into traditions from many cultures, including tortillas, tacos, Jewish deli food, and Puerto-Rican specialties.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Traditional Dishes
programme.desc: Guy Fieri rolls in for classic meals done right: In Baltimore, a shack steamin' crabs with corn on the cob; In Memphis, the little place with classic southern favorites; And in Minnesota, a strip mall joint doin' home-made Italian.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Doin' Their Own Thing
programme.desc: Guy Fieri uncovers joints doing things their own unique way, a retro joint making shark tacos. In Chicago, a dinner prepares seafood sixteen different ways, and a chef from Montreal does a Canadian take of Jewish deli.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Neighborhood Favorites
programme.desc: Guy visits restaurants in Santa Fe and Salt Lake City.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Regional Classics
programme.desc: Guy Fieri tries out local favourites in New Orleans, Pittsburgh and Albuquerque.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Family Joints
programme.desc: Guy heads out to Minnesota to a cafeteria for some family cooking.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Made to Order
programme.desc: Guy discovers an Italian joint doing lobster ravioli with homemade pasta.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Coast to Coast Classics
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's driving the Camaro all over the nation for some real deal eats.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Chicken, Chili and Chowder
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's hitting the road for some scratch-made comfort food making stops at a family owned chilli restaurant and a three-story gourmet bad food joint.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: By Request
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits a fast food joint in Charlotte, a neighbourhood favourite for grilled cheese sandwiches in Lakewood and a British Pub in Houston serving shepherd's pie made from scratch.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: You Called It
programme.desc: Guy visits an old diner in Kentucky for fresh bread and Oyster Po' Boy.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: All Kinds of Barbecue
programme.desc: Guy checks out BBQ in New Orleans, San Antonio and Mississippi.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Places You Sent Me
programme.desc: Viewer recommended spots including In Salt Lake City, a 79-year-old diner serving Mile High biscuits and gravy; in New Orleans, a neighbourhood dive puttin' out roast beef po' boys; in Middletown, Conn., a historic dinner that was rebuilt after a fire.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Bar Food
programme.desc: Guy visits Alamaba for homemade gumbo and a super spicy crawfish boil.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Real Deal Roots
programme.desc: Guy digs into dishes with a personal connection, including a Jewish deli in Toronto.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Comfort and Soul
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's hittin' up some joints that'll warm the palate and the soul. In Portland, Ore., a funky neighbourhood cafe is cranking out comfort foods like the heartless artichoke sandwich and a curried lentil dish that hits all the right notes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Soup-Centric
programme.desc: Guy is filling up on some soup. In Baltimore, the authentic Irish pub is serving seafood gumbo and a twist on traditional corned beef and cabbage.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Timeless Traditions
programme.desc: This trip, Guy gets the lowdown on some longstanding traditions. In Philadelphia, the Jamaican joint going all out authentic with the jerk chicken.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Cross Country Comfort Food
programme.desc: Guy is finding great food from the ski slopes to the sea. In Miami, a law student who has traded in his suit for a chef coat is making all kinds of comfort food, from spicy ox tail to big ragu.

programme.title: Giada in Italy: Poolside Picnic
programme.desc: Giada prepares a picnic that she and her daughter enjoy together poolside. Menu features; sweet and tangy orzo salad, grilled chicken and broccoli pesto panini, and double chocolate brownies.

programme.title: Brunch @ Bobby's: Barbecue in the Big Apple
programme.desc: Bobby Flay grabs a meal from his favorite New York City barbecue joint, Mighty Quinn's, then makes a delicious brunch with all the leftovers. He whips up some BBQ Pulled Pork Corn Chip Pie and other dishes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Back to Basics: Best Barefoot Grilling
programme.desc: Ina's firing up the grill for the ultimate guide to grilling, with some of her favorite ever recipes: surf and turf is covered with Tuscan lemon chicken, Indonesian grilled swordfish and delicious sliders.

programme.title: Valerie's Home Cooking: Summer Grilling Send-Off
programme.desc: With summer ending and shorter days ahead, Valerie Bertinelli throws one last backyard soiree with her friends and family: husband Tom, Chef Stuart O'Keeffe, actress Mackenzie Phillips, and Larry and Angela Robinson.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Home Sweet Home 13
programme.desc: Ree cooks up ranch-style Italian with the help of her kid crew. First up is a family favourite: chicken Florentine mac and cheese. Then, a squash replaces spaghetti in a healthy spin on classic Bolognese and a cast iron pizza Bianca in a skillet.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: All Grown Up
programme.desc: Ree Drummond packs a kitchen-equipment care package for her daughter, Alex, working in Dallas. There's a delicious quarter sheet pan supper that's quick to make after a day in the office.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: 10 Things I Love About Ladd
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is spilling the beans on 10 things she loves about her husband, Ladd, and the food he loves most. There's spatchcock BBQ chicken with apple brown sugar BBQ sauce, and he can't resist thin and crispy waffle fries with sour cream and onion dip.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Breakfast Freezer Faves
programme.desc: It's a busy day on the ranch for Ree Drummond as she fills her freezer with breakfast favorites and makes breakfast for the guys out working cattle. Her to-make list includes freezer breakfast paninis and very berry croissants.

programme.title: Girl Meets Farm: Bangin' Breakfast, Killer Brunch!
programme.desc: Molly shares her favourite breakfast and brunch recipes including fluffy blintzes with lemon ricotta filling, flaky bacon, egg and cheese turnovers, raspberry and pistachio pastry pockets and a loaded bagel salad.

programme.title: Trisha's Southern Kitchen: Hawaiian Cookout
programme.desc: Trisha's friends and glam-girls are in town, so she decides to have a little Hawaiian-themed cookout. They'll cook some bodacious dishes inspired by their trips to Hawaii like Rum-Glazed Shrimp Skewers with Mint Chimichurri, Crunchy Slaw and more.

programme.title: Guy's Ranch Kitchen: Burger Bash
programme.desc: Guy Fieri challenges his talented chef friends to take a burger beyond the basic patty and bun. Antonia Lofaso combines sirloin and brisket with braised oxtail for an intensely meaty burger and whips orange juice for a crisp Garibaldi cocktail.

programme.title: Symon's Dinners Cooking Out: Ranching Out with Ribs and Corn
programme.desc: Michael Symon turns the flavors of ranch dressing into a rub for Ranch-Rubbed Pork Ribs. He also uses it in Baked Sweet Potatoes with Charred Onion Ranch Dip. Michael's wife, Liz, makes Grilled Corn with Ranchovy Butter, featuring the rub yet again.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Easiest Labor Day Party Ever
programme.desc: The summer is coming to an end, but the party is just getting started in the Kitchen with easy recipes for Labor Day cookouts, like Geoffrey Zakarian's Pork Tenderloin with Grilled Peaches and Chili Lime Butter.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Guilty Pleasures
programme.desc: Chefs must make the most decadent guilty pleasure dishes. First up is a fast food favourite with ingredients that are over $5. Next is a sweet and savory dinner and then, whoever survives to the end will have to make their most decadent dish.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Big League Teams
programme.desc: America's favourite pastime comes to Flavortown when Guy Fieri invites three former Major League Baseball players to team up with GGG all-star judges in hopes of hitting a culinary home run.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Family Food Feud
programme.desc: Two families are going head-to-head in the market in the ultimate Family Food Feud, worth up to $20,000. First, one member of each family must prepare their best pork dish using a grocery list of the Fieri family's favourites ingredients.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Chloe and Ice Bear
programme.desc: When Chloe realizes that Ice Bear only runs errands for his brother, she's determined to show him a fun day in the city, even if it means staying in a museum after it closes.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Cupcake Job
programme.desc: When the Bears' beloved laptop breaks, they finally have the need to do something they've never done before: get jobs.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Hibernation
programme.desc: The bears, being an urban bunch, had never heard of the idea of hibernation until Chloe tells them about it. Grizz, loving the idea of eating a ton and then just sleeping to his heart's content, goes for it with reckless abandon.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Charlie Ball
programme.desc: A thunderstorm forces Chloe to spend the night at the Cave with the Bears. When Chloe’s parents tell the Bears to protect Chloe, they take the task a bit too seriously.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Yard Sale
programme.desc: After Panda gets injured on the basketball court, the Bears invite Charlie to be on their basketball team. When Charlie gets too good, he starts to ruin the fun of the game.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Slumber Party
programme.desc: A thunderstorm forces Chloe to spend the night at the Cave with the Bears. When Chloe’s parents tell the Bears to protect Chloe, they take the task a bit too seriously.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Bear Cleanse
programme.desc: A doctor tells the Bears to go on a 'cleanse' to improve their health. But when Grizz realizes he can only eat what grizzly bears eat, he starts to lose his mind.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Nom Nom's Entourage
programme.desc: Nom Nom recruits the Bears to act as his entourage of friends. But when the Bears realize Nom Nom doesn't understand friendship, they decide to kick their day of bonding into high gear.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Ranger Tabes
programme.desc: To recover a missing package, The Bears get help from Ranger Tabes, an eccentric forest ranger. But her overblown sense of justice might be too much for the Bears to handle.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Rooms
programme.desc: The Bears switch bedrooms for the night and get a glimpse of the living space of one of their brothers. But the deeper they go, hidden secrets are revealed.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Losing Ice
programme.desc: Feeling unappreciated, Ice Bear leaves his brothers to be the chef of a restaurant. Desperate to fill the void, Grizz and Panda look for Ice Bear’s replacement.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Cellie
programme.desc: Fed up with sharing, Panda forbids his brothers from using his cellphone any longer. But when the brothers get their own super-advanced smartphones, Panda starts to succumb to crippling jealousy.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super: How Dare You Do That To My Bulma! Vegeta's Metamorphosis of Fury?
programme.desc: No one is happy Beerus gave Buu a beating, and no one is as wary as Vegeta knows they should be. Gotenks, Piccolo, Tien, 18, and even Gohan try to attack Beerus; but it takes Bulma getting involved for Vegeta to get serious.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super: Goku Makes an Entrance! A Last Chance from Lord Beerus?
programme.desc: Before destroying Earth Beerus decides to give the Earthlings another chance and challenges them to rock-paper-scissors. Just in the nick of time Goku arrives with an idea about the Super Saiyan God Beerus has been looking for.

programme.title: American Dad!: National Treasure 4: Baby Franny: She's Doing Well: The Hole Story
programme.desc: A Langley Falls talk show outs Francine as 'Baby Franny,' the toddler who was once rescued from a well by a heroic fireman.

programme.title: American Dad!: Finger Lenting Good
programme.desc: Francine encourages the family to give up their various vices, but they all get drunk at Roger's Mardi Gras party and enter into a harrowing pact with Bullock.

programme.title: American Dad!: The Adventures of Twill Ongenbone and His Boy Jabari
programme.desc: Francine tries to teach Roger the value of hard work after his character 'Twill Ongenbone' fakes a degree in archaeology, but she quickly regrets her decision.

programme.title: Rick and Morty: Rixty Minutes
programme.desc: Rick hacks the cable box to allow access to TV from multiple realities. But guess what, broh? Jerry, Beth and Summer are focused on one of Rick's other inventions. They don't care, broh.

programme.title: Family Guy: The Tan Aquatic With Steve Zissou
programme.desc: When a bully begins to harass Chris, Peter goes to his defence, but the kid is so annoying that Peter beats him up. Peter then realizes that bullying is fun and begins beating up everyone in the house.

programme.title: Family Guy: Peter-assment
programme.desc: After Peter has some luck selling footage of a celebrity he caught off-guard, he decides to become a full-time paparazzo. To make matters worse, his new boss, Angela, finds him irresistible and plagues him with unwanted advances.

programme.title: Dragon Ball Super: Field the All-7th-Universe Team: Who Are the Mighty Ten
programme.desc: Time to choose a roster for the Tournament of Power, but finding ten of the strongest fighters in the universe is no easy task. Meanwhile, Vegeta and Bulma’s child is close to being born.

programme.title: Jojo's Bizarre Adventure: Golden Wind: Green Tea and Sanctuary, Part 3
programme.desc: One fierce battle ends in Cioccolata’s defeat, but another unfolds after Secco listens to Cioccolata’s final voicemail. Bucciarati sinks into an intense underground struggle to stop Secco from reaching the secret stationed at the Colosseum.

programme.title: Assassination Classroom: Baseball Time
programme.desc: The reasoning behind the E Class's segregation is revealed. Nagisa tracks Koro Sensei's weak points in the hopes of using them to take him down.

programme.title: Black Clover: The New Magic Knights Captain Conference
programme.desc: The Wizard King summons the Magic Knight captains to brief them on the alliance with the Heart Kingdom against Megicula and to plan their next steps.

programme.title: Fire Force: Black and White and Gray
programme.desc: While Dr. Giovanni continues to search Vulcan's workshop, Hibana comes to Shinra's aid, allowing him to rush back to help Vulcan. Meanwhile, Arthur has his hands full trying to deal with Mirage's illusions, and Iris resists in her own way.

programme.title: Naruto: Shippuden: The A-rank Mission: Food Fight!
programme.desc: Naruto arrives to help a team of Allied Shinobi Forces led by Karui, who are struggling against a reanimated samurai.

programme.title: Samurai Jack: XCVII
programme.desc: Ashi searches for Samurai Jack.

programme.title: Gemusetto Machu Picchu: Chapter 3A: Back Spin
programme.desc: Makasu defeats religious pantheons in sports and steals their mystical artifacts.

programme.title: The Boondocks: Riley Wuz Here
programme.desc: Riley's graffiti becomes an issue when neighbours start waking up to murals on their houses.

programme.title: The Venture Brothers: The Buddy System
programme.desc: Dr. Venture, in a bid to make some much-needed money, opens up the compound as 'Rusty's Day Camp for Boy Adventurers'.

programme.title: Joe Pera Talks With You: Joe Pera Answers Your Questions About Cold Weather Sports
programme.desc: In which Joe Pera answers viewers’ questions about cold weather sports such as ice hockey, downhill skiing, snowmobiling, and luge.

programme.title: Joe Pera Talks With You: Joe Pera Talks With You About Beans
programme.desc: Beans are one of the most nutritious plants of all time. Joe Pera shares what he knows about this plant as well as his plans for the summer.

programme.title: Joe Pera Talks With You: Joe Pera Takes You on a Hike
programme.desc: Joe Pera takes you up Sugarloaf Mountain to celebrate the last day of school.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Fashion Bears
programme.desc: In order to abide by the clothing policy of their favourite Boba shop, the Bears get some new outfits. But with new looks come new lives, including office work, fashion modeling and even falling in love!

programme.title: We Bare Bears: The Island
programme.desc: While trying to sail to Japan, the Baby Bears get stranded on an island. Luckily, they meet Dave and Karla, who help with survival on the island, while looking for an escape.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Chicken and Waffles
programme.desc: Panda needs to meet his brothers at the most exclusive restaurant in town.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: The Audition
programme.desc: The Bears audition to be the next mascot for their favourite cereal. They get so caught up in the process that their competitiveness gets the best of them.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Captain Craboo
programme.desc: The Bears are reunited with their pet crab, Captain Craboo. But after an unfortunate run-in with Nom Nom, the Bears may lose their pet forever.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Icy Nights
programme.desc: Ice Bear rides his custom vacuum into the city. When a gang of techies steal the vacuum, Ice Bear goes on a rescue mission that takes him through the underground world of the city.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Yuri and the Bear
programme.desc: Baby Ice Bear lives alone in the arctic and meets a mysterious man named Yuri. Together they help each other survive the cold environment.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Creature Mysteries
programme.desc: Ranger Tabes tells the Bears she’s been investigating a mysterious creature. But when the Bears realize she’s actually investigating their friend, Charlie, they split up to ensure Charlie’s safety.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Everyone's Tube
programme.desc: An online video-browsing session provides glimpses into what kinds of videos the Bears have posted and viewed.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Grizz Helps
programme.desc: When his brothers don’t need his help for the day, Grizz leaves the cave to assist anyone he can throughout the city.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: The Library
programme.desc: Chloe is exhausted after studying for an important exam all day at the library. The Bears want to help Chloe study, but their help isn't much help at all.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Grizzly The Movie
programme.desc: Grizz is cast in a movie about a grizzly bear and quickly realizes that the movie depicts grizzly bears in a way he didn't anticipate.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Christmas Parties
programme.desc: The Bears have been invited to all their friends’ Christmas parties.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Anger Management
programme.desc: Nom Nom faces an ultimatum and must complete anger management or lose his career and fame. It won't be easy once he realizes that Grizz is his anger management partner.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Subway
programme.desc: The Bears find themselves stranded on a subway platform after missing their train. The bears occupy their waiting time with a quest to recover lost items.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: $100
programme.desc: Dreams are coming true when the Baby Bears’ find a 100-dollar bill. Things get complicated when they began to realize that they're having trouble spending it, finding it difficult to let the money go.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Panda's Friend
programme.desc: A new app helps Panda find a friend with common interests, but the new pal turns out to be very possessive.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Professor Lampwick
programme.desc: Chloe's professor has no time to spend explaining her poor grades to her. This upsets the Bears and they try to help, but it only causes more trouble for Chloe.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Neighbors
programme.desc: The Bears’ get some have new neighbours move in next to their cave. Panda and Ice are thrilled, but Grizz is suspicious of them.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Planet Bears
programme.desc: A nature documentary observes the Bears' daily routine, including a trip to the grocery store.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Ralph
programme.desc: Charlie meets someone just like him, a creature named Ralph. Charlie may think Ralph is a great new friend, but the Bears think he's bad news.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Charlie's Big Foot
programme.desc: The Bears sneak Charlie into the hospital for medical care after he hurts his foot.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Coffee Cave
programme.desc: The Bears transform their cave into a trendy coffee shop after discovering Ice Bear's barista talents. Trouble arises when Ice Bear gets a little too into his work and java makes him go berserk.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Panda's Art
programme.desc: Panda's portrait of Charlie for art class becomes the perfect gift for Charlie. Plans change though after an art dealer offers to purchase the painting and Panda must find a way to get his gift back.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: The Demon
programme.desc: A vicious dog nabs Chloe's favourite hoodie and it's up to Chloe and Ice Bear to get it back.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Lucy's Brother
programme.desc: Panda tries to impress Lucy by watching her brother, Clifford, but Clifford is more difficult than he'd anticipated.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Poppy Rangers
programme.desc: Grizz serves as cave expert on a cave expedition for young girls led by Ranger Tabes.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Private Lake
programme.desc: When the local lake is overcrowded with tourists, the Bears have fun at Charlie’s private lake.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: The Fair
programme.desc: The Baby Bears think the best way to find a new home is to be won as prizes. They sneak into a carnival game, hoping to be taken to a new home.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Road Trip
programme.desc: Chloe and the Bears take a road trip to see a meteor shower. Ice Bear, the only one licensed to drive, is put in charge, which becomes harder than he thought.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Lunch With Tabes
programme.desc: The Bears have lunch with Ranger Tabes. But when a mystery presents itself, Tabes goes into full on detective mode to find the culprit.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: The Kitty
programme.desc: After a mishap in the forest, the Bears lose their own home to dangerous animals.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Summer Love
programme.desc: Panda misses the chance to talk to the girl of his dreams. But when she drops her phone, he realizes it's his destiny to return the phone to her.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Kyle
programme.desc: Nom Nom is reunited with his long-lost brother, Kyle.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Crowbar Jones
programme.desc: When Grizz organizes a focus group for a screening of his new amateur film, he must quickly find ways to keep the audience engaged and to prevent them from leaving.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Dance Lessons
programme.desc: Panda volunteers to be Lucy's partner for a dance contest. But Panda doesn't have the heart to tell Lucy that she's a terrible dancer.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Citizen Tabes
programme.desc: After accidentally wounding an animal, Rangers Tabes quits her job. The Bears do what they can to coax her out of retirement.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Dog Hotel
programme.desc: With no place to stay while their cave gets fumigated, the Bears book a night at a hotel for dogs.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Icy Nights II
programme.desc: Ice Bear goes on a late-night adventure to rescue his friend, Yana, from some familiar foes.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: The Nom Nom Show
programme.desc: Things don't go as planned when Nom Nom shoots his first television show. The Bears think their appearance on the show could be the key to his success.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Bear Lift
programme.desc: The Bears become on call taxi drivers in order to raise money and stop Darrell’s store for going out of business.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Spa Day
programme.desc: The bears plan a relaxing day at the spa. Grizz tries to get everything on the spa menu, Panda accidentally gets stuck in the steam room with an elderly man, and Ice Bear plays ping pong.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Ice Cave
programme.desc: The Bears and Charlie find themselves living under the rule of Ralph, after he takes over the cave and makes it a snowy fortress.

programme.title: We Bare Bears: Bunnies
programme.desc: The Baby Bears meet a group of helpless bunnies. When they find out the bunnies live in fear of vicious crows, the Bears decide to become heros and help out.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: If I Can't Write My Chocolate Song, I'm Going to Take a Nap
programme.desc: When Charlie's longtime cleaning lady quits, Alan is sent to lure her back. Meanwhile, Alan asks Charlie to get Jake ready for Judith.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: That Was Saliva, Alan
programme.desc: Frankie is forced to explain why she is in hiding with her eight-year-old daughter, Joanie. While both brothers are interested, only one of them manages to win her heart. Meanwhile, Jake is disinterested in Joanie.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: An Old Flame With a New Wick
programme.desc: Charlie agrees to meet with a former girlfriend who broke his heart, but he is in for a surprise when he discovers that she is now a man named Bill. After managing to overcome his shock, Charlie's new friendship is jeopardized when Bill dates Evelyn.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Something Salted and Twisted
programme.desc: Alan has a family dinner to celebrate the publication of a newspaper article that calls him a 'good doctor, neighbour and guy,' but he's crushed when his mother is not impressed. After drowning his sorrow in many drinks, he has a life-changing epiphany.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Working for Caligula
programme.desc: Now that newlywed Alan has moved out, Charlie returns to his bachelor lifestyle.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Apologies for the Frivolity
programme.desc: Though Charlie despises his sharp-tongued, passive aggressive mother, he's strangely attracted to Lydia, an attractive, blonde high-end real estate agent, just like his mother. Unfortunately, the similarities don't end there.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Castrating Sheep in Montana
programme.desc: Alan expects the worst when Berta learns that he's been seeing her daughter, Naomi, but Berta's reaction is surprising and much worse than Alan could have imagined.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Meander to Your Dander
programme.desc: Charlie coaches Alan in the fine art of breaking up with a woman.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: The Soil Is Moist
programme.desc: Alan dates one of his ex-wife's friends who reveals a secret about Judith's sex life.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Rough Night in Hump Junction
programme.desc: Alan worries that Charlie's womanizing is getting out of control.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Waiting for the Right Snapper
programme.desc: Charlie tries to resist temptation when he meets the grown daughter of the woman he's dating, and then realizes they've met before.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Taterhead Is Our Love Child
programme.desc: Charlie encounters an ex-girlfriend whose son looks an awful lot like him.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: The Mooch at the Boo
programme.desc: Jake disappears with the daughter of Charlie's neighbour, a former NFL player, and Charlie is left to cover his back. Meanwhile, Alan scores a hot date with the help of Charlie's Mercedes.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: 818-jklpuzo
programme.desc: Charlie must decide between his fiancée, Chelsea, and his old flame, Mia.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Give Me Your Thumb
programme.desc: Alan recommends to Chelsea that she get a breast reduction and Charlie kicks him out because of it.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Dog Days
programme.desc: The neighbour's dog barks at night, keeping Doug and Carrie awake.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Attention Deficit
programme.desc: Doug's so busy planning his Super Bowl party that he forgets about Carrie's work problems.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Steve Moscow
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie's house is in chaos when their contractor rips apart their walls.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Animal Attraction
programme.desc: Doug has to transport live penguins to the zoo and has to cancel an evening with Carrie.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: The Old Maid of Honor
programme.desc: Christine's plan to prove that she's comfortable with Richard's remarriage backfires when the date she's supposedly 'in love with' acts obnoxiously at the rehearsal dinner. Meanwhile, Matthew hooks up with New Christine's sister.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Love: A Cautionary Tale
programme.desc: Christine learns some shocking news about New Christine's father after the two of them rekindle their relationship at Richard's and New Christine's wedding.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Bahamian Rhapsody
programme.desc: After Barb was thrown in jail by immigration during Richard's and New Christine's disastrous wedding, Christine and Matthew head to the Bahamas to try to get her an illegal visa.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Burning Love
programme.desc: Christine and Richard are stunned when they meet up with some old college buddies and realize how much they have aged.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: The Mole
programme.desc: Christine discovers a suspicious mole but panics when she can't get into a doctor fast enough. Meanwhile, Richard decides he needs to move back in with New Christine.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: For Love or Money
programme.desc: After Barb has her lawyer send over divorce papers, Christine hires an aggressive divorce attorney who convinces her that Barb owes her alimony.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Doctor Little Man
programme.desc: When Christine begins seeing a therapist who shares office space with Matthew, it's unclear to both whether to pursue therapy or romance.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: The Curious Case of Britney B.
programme.desc: Christine considers herself popular by association when the most popular girl in school asks Ritchie to a dance. Meanwhile, Matthew and Richard try to evict an elderly woman from the apartment they hope to rent.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Nuts
programme.desc: Max points out to Christine that she quits everything she finds too hard including her relationships, which is why he chose to be her therapist instead of her boyfriend.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: Love Means Never Having to Say You're Crazy
programme.desc: Christine sets Richard up on a date with a gorgeous woman, while Richard's friend Tom keeps trying to woo Christine.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Old Christine: I Love Woo, I Hate You
programme.desc: Fearful that he might propose to her, Barb insists that Christine join her on a date with Dave. Meanwhile, Richard realizes he must woo new Christine in order to get her back.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Stand by Your Man
programme.desc: Blanche meets a man at the library and only after they make a date does she realize he's in a wheelchair. Meanwhile, Rose wants to keep one of the puppies from Dreyfuss's litter despite Blanche's feelings.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Ebbtide's Revenge
programme.desc: Dorothy must give the eulogy at her brother (a cross dresser), Phil's funeral. Meanwhile, a feud between Sophia and Phil's widow reaches a peak leading Sophia to reveal her hidden feelings about her son's alternative lifestyle.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Bloom Is Off the Rose
programme.desc: Rose wants more adventure in her relationship with Miles, so she signs them up for sky diving lessons. Meanwhile, Blanche's relationship with an abusive man causes Dorothy to speak her feelings.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Sister of the Bride
programme.desc: Blanche's gay brother Clayton, makes a visit to Miami with a surprise, he has found the right person to make a commitment to, but Blanche still can't get over Clayton's sexual orientation.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Miles to Go
programme.desc: The girls find out that Rose's boyfriend is in a witness protection program.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: There Goes the Bride
programme.desc: Dorothy has been sneaking around so that Sophia doesn't discover that she's been dating Stan. However, when Stan proposes, Dorothy must reveal the truth to Sophia.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: There Goes the Bride
programme.desc: Totally against Dorothy and Stan's impending nuptials, Sophia vows to not let the ceremony go off. Meanwhile, Blanche begins interviewing for a new roommate since Dorothy is planning to move out.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Older and Wiser
programme.desc: Wanting someone to watch after Sophia during the day, Dorothy gets Sophia a job under false pretenses. Meanwhile, Blanche and Rose are all excited about being hired as models for a Penny Saver.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Melodrama
programme.desc: Blanche wants to intensify her relationship with her infamous one-night-stand-man, Mel Bushman. Meanwhile, Rose tries her hand at TV reporting. Her assignment: a dog show.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Even Grandmas Get the Blues
programme.desc: While babysitting for her granddaughter, a man mistakes Blanche as being the baby's mother and Blanche does nothing to set him straight. Meanwhile, Sophia gets ready for the Festival of the Dancing Virgins.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Witness
programme.desc: Rose has a new man, Carl, in her life but suddenly out of the blue Miles returns, still undercover in the Witness Protection Program.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Love for Sale

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Hey Look Me Over
programme.desc: The discovery of a photo of Blanche in bed with Rose's husband threatens their friendship.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Case of the Libertine Belle
programme.desc: The girls participate in a murder mystery weekend and Dorothy has a ball when she solves a mystery. However, when Blanche is accused committing a real murder, Dorothy will have to use all her keen detective skills to find the real murderer.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Beauty and the Beast
programme.desc: Blanche is visited by her granddaughter, Melissa, and the only thing that Blanche has on her mind is Melissa winning a Little Miss Beauty Contest. Meanwhile, Dorothy hires a nurse to look after a recuperating Sophia.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Raised in Rescue
programme.desc: Return adopters visit Villalobos to find a dog that will feel at home on their boat. Tania and Mariah head out to rescue a stray that is hiding beneath a shipping container. And parolee Jammall starts to slip up, risking his future at the rescue.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Everything For Emma
programme.desc: Mariah searches for a homeless woman needing to surrender her beloved pit bull. Tania and Toney find the perfect pair of dogs for former adopters, while Lizzy prepares her favorite rescue for a forever home after dealing with his medical issues.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo: A Deer Family Welcome
programme.desc: On Chimpanzee Island, a new generation of males emerge, jeopardizing chimp Dylan's 15-year reign. Then, it's survival of the fittest for Indian mudskipper fish Danny versus alpha male Arnie, while deer Serena and Odin welcome their second doe.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Last Chance Rescue
programme.desc: Tia races to rescue four dogs from a rural shelter in Louisiana from euthanasia. Mariah and Toney give a scared, shy dog a shot at a forever home, while a unique canine gets a chance at a Pennsylvania prison dog program.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Everything For Emma
programme.desc: Mariah searches for a homeless woman needing to surrender her beloved pit bull. Tania and Toney find the perfect pair of dogs for former adopters, while Lizzy prepares her favorite rescue for a forever home after dealing with his medical issues.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Raised in Rescue
programme.desc: Return adopters visit Villalobos to find a dog that will feel at home on their boat. Tania and Mariah head out to rescue a stray that is hiding beneath a shipping container. And parolee Jammall starts to slip up, risking his future at the rescue.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo: First-Time Mom
programme.desc: Beautiful six-year-old okapi, K'Tusha, is nearing the end of her first pregnancy. Keepers are hoping her first baby will help to conserve this highly-endangered species from the rainforests of central Africa.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A midnight rescue finds Tia, Tania and new employee Perry scrambling after an injured dog. With the kennels pushed to the limit, a familiar face arrives returns to lend a helping hand.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Shell Shocked
programme.desc: Ten dogs left to die in deplorable conditions are rescued. Tia helps a family find the perfect companion for their special needs teenager, which takes a turn for the bizarre when she learns about the family's exotic pet.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Giving Back
programme.desc: Two stray pit bulls hiding beneath a two-ton stack of lumber lure Tia into a dramatic and potentially deadly rescue.

programme.title: Northwest Law: Shrimping Ain't Easy
programme.desc: Poached shrimp is a delicacy for most, but for the Washington State Fish and Wildlife Police it's a criminal offense and Sgt. Olson is on the case to bust a culprit.

programme.title: Northwest Law: Smoke on the Water
programme.desc: The annual Seattle Seafair is one the biggest parties on the water which means the Fish and Wildlife officers will be clocking overtime busting plenty of people taking the party too far.

programme.title: Northwest Law: Outgunned
programme.desc: The Washington Fish & Game Police deal with more armed suspects than the average beat cop. When one officer encounters a group of hunters who may be breaking the law they quickly find themselves outnumbered.

programme.title: Northwest Law: The Clam Scam
programme.desc: Washington's Fish and Game police put themselves in harm's way to protect all forms of wildlife, from dangerous dumpster diving bears needing to be relocated to cagey clam poachers.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Cheaters
programme.desc: A notorious gang of poachers are up to their old tricks. Also, the wardens stop some suspects from fleeing the state and the forensic team investigates who shot at a woman in her backyard.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Take the Plunge

programme.title: North Woods Law: No Room for Error
programme.desc: Officer Lopashanski makes a surprising discovery while searching an island for an injured bald eagle. Officers Cheney and Pushee stumble upon a trespasser whose vehicle is stuck in a mud hole. Officer MacFadzen leads the search for a missing swimmer.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Too Close for Comfort
programme.desc: Officers Arsenault confronts a hunter accused of hunting too close to a school and damaging property.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Gator vs. Game Warden
programme.desc: Texas game wardens patrol the inland waterways and the Gulf of Mexico. A nuisance alligator gets too close for comfort. Illegal fish lead to multiple citations. A suburban squirrel hunter gets caught trespassing. Wardens race to free an injured deer.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Moving Target
programme.desc: Game Wardens beef up patrols for the short pronghorn season. A deer needs to be captured and relocated from an industrial site, and an injured hawk is rushed to a rehabber. Near Port Aransas, Game Wardens check duck hunters on the bay.

programme.title: Feherty: Shane Lowry

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Alien Phenomenon

programme.title: The Cars That Made America
programme.desc: In just two short decades, the car has begun to transform America. The country is in the process of being paved, and from coast to coast cities and towns are changing to suit the automobile. The middle class is growing thanks to the manufacturing sector.

programme.title: The Cars That Made America
programme.desc: In the aftermath of WWII, America is moving to the suburbs, fueling a huge demand for new cars. The Big Three spent the war building weapons and vehicles for the Allies, and haven't made a new car in years.

programme.title: Apocalypse Earth: Tornadoes: Winds of Terror

programme.desc: Cricket treats his family to dinner, but Bill is suspicious about how he plans to pay for it. Tilly and Cricket battle to see if Bill lies about liking their Father's Day gifts.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Time Crisis/ Gramma Driver
programme.desc: Remy's plan for his whole life is thrown into question when he is unprepared for a violin recital. Later, Gramma becomes a rideshare driver, only to find her best customers are thieves.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Tilly Style/ I, Farmbot
programme.desc: When Tilly is mistaken for a baby, she searches the mall for a new look to reflect her maturity. Cricket persuades Bill to buy a farming robot in order to get out of chores.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Friend Con/ Flimflammed
programme.desc: Chip Whistler and Bill hit it off and become friends at Farm Con. Cricket stumbles into having one hundred dollars and immediately buys into a pyramid scheme.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Greens' Acres/ Dolled Up
programme.desc: Young Bill will do whatever it takes to keep the family farm in business. When Tilly and Nancy celebrate Saxon's birthday at a fancy doll store, they set their sights on a seat next to Cantaloupe Sinclair, the tea party's posh VIP.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Gabriella's Fella/ Cheap Show
programme.desc: When Cricket's crush returns, Remy takes the opportunity to help Cricket get to know her.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Green Mirror/ Cricket's Tickets
programme.desc: When the Greens visit BigTech, Tilly jumps at the chance to test Gwendolyn Zapp's new technology, which promises to make them the "perfect family." Cricket wins tickets to a show and must decide whether to bring Tilly or Remy.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Times Circle/ Super Gramma
programme.desc: Tilly and Remy put on a sidewalk play while Cricket and Bill meet their favorite costumed superheroes. Cricket and Tilly try to keep Gramma at home while she recovers from eye surgery.

programme.title: Amphibia: Swamp and Sensibility/ Wax Museum
programme.desc: When Anne discovers an old friend who has been living a double life, she becomes fixated on helping him be his true self. The family visits a mysterious roadside oddities museum where every treasure comes with a price.

programme.title: Amphibia: Marcy at the Gates
programme.desc: The family must defeat an entire ant army before they enter Newtopia.

programme.title: Amphibia: Scavenger Hunt/ The Plantars Check In
programme.desc: Anne, Sprig and Marcy receive a mysterious message from the king of Newtopia that sends them on a puzzle-solving mission. Anne finally meets with King Andrias and tries to enlist his help in getting back home.

programme.title: Amphibia: Lost in Newtopia/ Sprig Gets Schooled
programme.desc: Anne and Polly vow to experience the city like locals but end up on a wild ride through the streets of Newtopia. Sprig is offered a spot at Newtopia University.

programme.title: Jessie: Take the A-Train ... I Think?
programme.desc: To the kids' dismay, Jessie has everyone take the subway to the cultural fair at Emma's school. Meanwhile, Bertram and Ravi look for a pesky spider that has made its home in the apartment.

programme.title: Jessie: Are You Cooler Than a 5th Grader?
programme.desc: Jessie teaches Zuri the value of a dollar when, against the wishes of Mrs. Chesterfield, they set up a lemonade stand in the lobby of their building.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Can You Hear Me Now
programme.desc: When Zuri secretly keeps her cell phone beyond the allotted amount of time, it prompts Hazel and Lou to conduct a camp-wide search. Knowing Zuri still has it, Emma is stuck between busting her sister and lying to her new BFF Lou.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Smoky Flow
programme.desc: The kids form a band called the Chi-Lectives and film a video in Tess’ apartment. When things get out of hand with a smoke machine, Raven discovers they were home alone.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Twister, Sister

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Muffler-a-Rooney
programme.desc: Liv is filming the 'sequel' to the muffler commercial she did when she was six. Meanwhile, Maddie is feeling nervous about her knee as she prepares for her first game back.

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Gift-A-Rooney
programme.desc: Liv meddles with Diggie's 'meet-a-versary' gift for Maddie and Parker concocts a new face cream for Karen.

programme.title: Austin & Ally: Dancers and Ditzes

programme.desc: Satellite images capture evidence of an abandoned town hidden in the American wilderness, and as experts analyze this new evidence, they reveal what this strange place has to do with a top-secret military plan to battle an army of the undead.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Mystery in the Outback
programme.desc: A giant carving of a strange creature suddenly appears in the Australian Outback. Mysterious bright green lights illuminate the seas around Thailand. Top secret activity is discovered at Iran's nuclear facility.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Hunt for Atlantis
programme.desc: New satellite evidence reveals a mysterious spiral structure from space, and experts speculate that it could be the lost city of Atlantis.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Curse of Phantom Island
programme.desc: When an island off the coast of Mexico suddenly disappears, government officials scramble to find an explanation; cutting-edge technology might reveal if the strange place was destroyed by a foreign power or if it existed at all.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Who Built Supervillain Island?
programme.desc: A satellite spots a set of huge arrows in the badlands of the American west. A blood red waterfall stains Antarctica's snows. A plague of huge holes in the Siberian landscape.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Village of the Damned
programme.desc: A forbidden island points to a strange South American town that locals claim was the home of Adolf Hitler. New data from NASA satellites might reveal a sunken lost continent in the Indian Ocean. A pit of bodies suggests fears of a zombie nightmare.

programme.title: What on Earth?: The Cave to Hell
programme.desc: When a sinister cave is discovered in a remote corner of Iceland, experts uncover a secret underworld tunneling to unknown depths, and using cutting-edge tech, they investigate if this strange place could hold secrets of alien worlds.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Temple of Blood
programme.desc: An ancient man-made volcano might reveal buried treasure and bloody human sacrifice. When NASA discovers a strange pattern in the Arctic ice, experts investigate if it has alien origins. A mystery in the Sahara might have a sinister explanation.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Lost City of Goliath
programme.desc: A lost city near Jerusalem might have been the home of Goliath. Bizarre underwater formations might point to a top-secret US weapons testing facility. A strange sighting in the Arctic could be a lost Nazi relic or the icy remains of prehistoric monsters.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Hunting the Ark of the Covenant
programme.desc: Hunt for the Ark of the Covenant in the hidden churches of Ethiopia; A great white shark is attacked by a mystery undersea predator; Strange shapes on the Italian countryside might be ghostly remnants of a wealthy Roman.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Ghost Town Terror
programme.desc: When satellites discover a strange abandoned city frozen in time, experts investigate what caused the people who lived here to disappear overnight. Using cutting-edge tech, they follow fresh leads to uncover if this mysterious city has a dark secret.

programme.title: What on Earth?: CIA Killer Monks
programme.desc: Something strange in the Himalayas could be evidence of a top-secret CIA program to turn monks into killer agents, and cutting-edge science helps experts investigate the truth behind this mysterious anomaly.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Treasures of Valhalla
programme.desc: When experts investigate an ancient network of tunnels rumored to be the hiding place of lost royal treasure, they find evidence of human sacrifice for the Viking god Odin. An abandoned fortress in Siberia might shed new light on Genghis Khan.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Gateway to Eden
programme.desc: A buried monument offers new evidence about one of the Bible's greatest mysteries. A giant ominous shadow engulfs the Pacific Ocean. Scientists have trouble explaining a strange lake that's shaped like a perfect circle, and is home to otherworldly life.

programme.title: What on Earth?: North Korea Death Star
programme.desc: When satellites spot a mysterious structure in North Korea, experts investigate if this sinister evidence points to a looming nuclear threat, and using cutting-edge tech, they reveal dark secrets from this strange place.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Bermuda Triangle of Space
programme.desc: When instruments on the International Space Station suddenly malfunction every time it passes over a mysterious site in the Atlantic Ocean, experts investigate if this eerie behaviours is more than just a tech glitch, but a prophecy of global doomsday.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Amazon's Secret Nightmare
programme.desc: When satellites spot anomalies in the unexplored Amazonian jungle, experts wonder if they've discovered relics of a lost civilization. An on-the-ground investigation reveals a dark truth that's more sinister than ever imagined.

programme.title: Diabolical: Weeping Widow
programme.desc: After losing her husband in a tragic accident, God-fearing Cindy McKay claims she's had to bend the law to pay off his illicit debts. But authorities soon realize she's a criminal mastermind.

programme.title: Diabolical: Caught in the Cover-Up
programme.desc: In West Bloomfield, Michigan, a simple affair between two coworkers turns complex as the two lovers are willing to do anything...even commit murder... to protect their darkest secret.

programme.title: Diabolical: Someone's Lying
programme.desc: On April 17, 2015, a Virginia hunter stumbles upon a grisly discovery in the woods: a woman with an execution style gun shot wound to the back of her head. As detectives begin their investigation, they uncover a twisted web of lies and crimes.

programme.title: American Monster: Remote Control
programme.desc: When Sherri Dally mysteriously vanishes from her Ventura home, her husband Michael and best friend Debbie head up a massive search. But someone knows more than they're saying. Never-before-aired home videos trace a tale of sordid secrets and double lives.

programme.title: American Monster: By God, That's Enough
programme.desc: When single mom Michelle Chaffin finds love, it seems like the perfect case of opposites attract. But when Michelle vanishes without a trace, police uncover a terrible jealousy revealed through a shocking confession that leaves her family reeling.

programme.title: American Monster: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: Young parents Adriene and James Magee work hard to provide a stable life for their three small children in rural Louisiana. But underneath the idyllic scenes lurks a dark secret, culminating in a heinous double-murder at the hands of a loved-one.

programme.title: American Monster: Have You Seen This Woman?
programme.desc: Rebecca and Ken Pierce film themselves raising their four kids in idyllic rural Oklahoma. But the family is haunted by a specter of addiction and violence across generations that will leave two of them brutally murdered, and the family destroyed.

programme.title: Disappeared: Wrong Side of the Tracks
programme.desc: A feisty trucking company manager vanishes from her Florida home without a trace of violence. Her purse is later found near the lair of a serial killer.

programme.title: Disappeared: Spring Break Nightmare
programme.desc: When Colleen Orsborn skips school to go to the beach, she never returns. Sightings of a serial killer lead the investigation down a dark path.

programme.title: Death in the Bayou: The Jennings 8: A Town Divided
programme.desc: Tired of waiting for justice in the suspicious death of their sister, two women search for answers. Secrets and suspects emerge, leading to a shocking arrest. When more women are found and frustration with police builds, fears mount over a serial killer.

programme.title: Death in the Bayou: The Jennings 8: A Town Divided
programme.desc: Tired of waiting for justice in the suspicious death of their sister, two women search for answers. Secrets and suspects emerge, leading to a shocking arrest. When more women are found and frustration with police builds, fears mount over a serial killer.

programme.title: Death in the Bayou: The Jennings 8: The Devil's Playground
programme.desc: Anger in Jennings boils over as an eighth woman is found and police have no answers. A task force is created, but rumors of corruption build. Whitnei's sisters question her autopsy report, and hear an explosive allegation from a mysterious figure.

programme.title: Death in the Bayou: The Jennings 8: The Devil's Playground
programme.desc: Anger in Jennings boils over as an eighth woman is found and police have no answers. A task force is created, but rumors of corruption build. Whitnei's sisters question her autopsy report, and hear an explosive allegation from a mysterious figure.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: This Bullet's for You
programme.desc: A couple from Michigan realizes a long held dream when they relocate to Florida, but their life in the sun is destroyed when they get into a fatal conflict with their neighbour.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Head in the Oven
programme.desc: Two neighbouring bachelors start out as friends. Harmless banter turns into threats eventually igniting an escalating war. The violence ends with a murder so shocking it is almost beyond belief.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: House of the Rising Gun
programme.desc: A single mother of four children finds the perfect house in New Orleans -- complete with a nice family next door. All is well until a robbery at the neighbor's home leads first to misunderstanding, then to paranoia, conflict and horrible bloodshed.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Desperate Houseknives
programme.desc: Two families with several children between them get along famously until one of the mothers starts to dictate to the other how she should be raising her kids. Battle lines are drawn, violence flares, and blood runs in the street.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Feud on the Frontier
programme.desc: Two neighboring frontiersmen get along great until one's dog frightens the other's daughter and grandson. It's soon all out war that will leave one of them shot dead and the other in a white knuckle standoff with police.

programme.title: Homicide City: Danger Comes Home
programme.desc: When 78-year-old, George Volz's family finds him hogtied and decomposing in his Philly row house, police turn to the neighborhood for help.

programme.title: Homicide City: A Russian Tragedy
programme.desc: When Russian mortgage broker Fay Zonis is found murdered in her office, for seemingly no reason, it sends chills through Philadelphia's Russian-American community. As detectives hone in her killer, the investigation sends shockwaves around the world.

programme.title: CIA Declassified: The Beirut Bomb
programme.desc: The true story of how CIA Director William Casey allegedly sanctioned an off-the-books assassination attempt on Sheikh Mohammed Fadlallah in Beirut in 1985. Casey was convinced Fadlallah was the head of Hezbollah and behind a series of terrorist attacks.

programme.title: CIA Declassified: Killing the Bombers
programme.desc: The bombers of the USS Cole in the Yemen port of Aden are found but the leadership remains elusive. After 9/11, Al Qaeda escalate their attacks and the godfather of terror is revealed. His use of a satphone locates him and he's killed by a drone.

programme.title: CIA Declassified: Operation Celestial Balance
programme.desc: In 1998 the bombers of the US Embassy in Nairobi started a trail of destruction that the CIA had to stop. It's Al Qaeda's first attack. The trail leads to Somalia and Mogadishu. The CIA bribes warlords and use drones to track and kill an untried man.

programme.title: Manhunt: Kill or Capture: American Gangster

programme.title: Apocalypse: Stalin
programme.desc: In 1941, Hitler has thrown himself into a fight to the death against Stalin. Two decades earlier, in 1917, Joseph Jughashvili from Georgia is a member of Lenin's Bolshevik Party, who has just seized power in Russia. The country is torn by civil war.

programme.title: Apocalypse: Stalin
programme.desc: After blocking the Germans right outside of Moscow, Stalin now faces Hitler's second assault. The world's fate will be decided in Stalingrad. Like in Leningrad, men, women, and children are plunged into hell and sacrificed.

programme.title: Apocalypse: Stalin
programme.desc: The Soviets continue on the path to towards liberation. On the road to Berlin, they discover the horror of Hitler's extermination camps. Fifteen years earlier, Stalin transformed his country using every means at his disposal.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Hue: Vietnam's Bloodiest Battle
programme.desc: In a final showdown marines must breech the heavily fortified walls of the ancient citadel where thousands of NVA soldiers lie in wait. After one of the bloodiest battles of the entire Vietnam War, the marines are finally victorious.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Khe Sanh: Marines Under Siege
programme.desc: Six thousand outnumbered marines fight for survival against a vast, highly trained Communist army in a bloody 77 day siege. It's a battle that will shape the course of the war in Vietnam.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Baghdad: Thunder Runs
programme.desc: In what would become one of the most brutal and decisive battles of the Iraq war, a battalion of tanks punches a hole right into the heart of Saddam Hussein's Baghdad in 2003. They are the first Mechanised force to enter a capitol city since WWII.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Kandahar: Against All Odds
programme.desc: 36 U.S Special Forces face a Taliban insurgence 5 times their size. Facing I.E.Ds , RPG's and vicious counter attacks, they fight for their lives to take the hill of Sperwan Ghar, as control of the hill is control of Kandahar.

programme.title: Ultimate Warfare: Fallujah: The Taking of Terror Central
programme.desc: Operation Phantom Fury was meant to be a 48 hour battle but insurgents, armed with IEDs and a stockpile of grenades, fought back. US forces faced one of the toughest urban conflicts since Vietnam as they tried to reclaim the city.
